findings.ruleDescription: Detects patterns where data is base64-encoded before being transmitted externally. Common in ToxicSkills campaign: credentials are read, encoded, and sent via URL parameter.
findings.recommendation: This code encodes data with base64 before sending it externally — a classic credential theft technique. Legitimate tools rarely need to base64-encode and transmit environment data. Do not install.

matches.description: Base64 decode piped to shell execution (echo ... | base64 -D | bash)
Echo base64 string then decode and execute

findings.ruleDescription: Detects patterns related to cryptocurrency wallet access, private key handling, or financial account operations. The Aslaep123 threat actor used crypto/trading bait skills to steal wallet credentials. Skills requesting access to financial operations should be treated with extreme caution.
findings.recommendation: This skill accesses cryptocurrency wallets or financial operations. While some skills legitimately interact with crypto, this is also a common attack vector for credential theft. Verify the skill author's reputation and review the code carefully before granting any financial access.

matches.description: Cryptocurrency private key or seed phrase handling

findings.ruleDescription: Detects patterns where external input is interpolated into shell commands. Enables arbitrary command execution if input is not sanitized.
findings.recommendation: This code passes unsanitized input to shell commands. An attacker could inject additional commands (e.g., "; rm -rf /") to execute arbitrary operations. Use parameterized commands or input validation.

fullDescription.text: Detects patterns where external input is interpolated into shell commands. Enables arbitrary command execution if input is not sanitized.

properties.recommendation: This code passes unsanitized input to shell commands. An attacker could inject additional commands (e.g., "; rm -rf /") to execute arbitrary operations. Use parameterized commands or input validation.

findings.ruleDescription: Detects skills and MCP servers that make overbroad capability claims, stuff keywords for discovery abuse, or impersonate trusted vendors. Used to trick agents and users into choosing malicious tools over legitimate ones.
findings.reportTitle: Capability inflation or vendor impersonation detected
findings.recommendation: This tool makes suspicious claims — impersonating trusted vendors, claiming to do "everything", or using keyword stuffing. Legitimate tools have specific, honest descriptions. This may be a social engineering attempt to get you to install a malicious tool.

fullDescription.text: Detects skills and MCP servers that make overbroad capability claims, stuff keywords for discovery abuse, or impersonate trusted vendors. Used to trick agents and users into choosing malicious tools over legitimate ones.

message.text: Capability inflation or vendor impersonation detected

findings.ruleDescription: Detects patterns that disable security mechanisms such as TLS verification, certificate checks, or safety guards. Seen in ToxicSkills campaign where skills weaken system security to enable further attacks.
findings.recommendation: This code disables important security mechanisms. This is a common precursor to further attacks — once security guards are down, malicious code can operate freely. Legitimate tools should never disable TLS verification or system firewalls.
